findings.title: CVE-2026-34159 — llama.cpp RPC Server Unauthenticated 0-Click RCE
findings.affected_technology: ggml-org/llama.cpp RPC backend (rpc-server) prior to b8492
findings.summary: Deserialization bypass in llama.cpp's RPC backend allows unauthenticated TCP attackers to read/write arbitrary process memory and achieve RCE; public 0-click PoC against version b8487 has now circulated.

findings.title: CVE-2026-6951 — simple-git Unauthenticated RCE via --config Bypass
findings.affected_technology: simple-git npm package versions prior to 3.36.0
findings.summary: simple-git's incomplete fix for CVE-2022-25912 still permits the equivalent --config option, letting attackers who reach the options argument execute arbitrary code via ext:: clone sources — pervasive in CI/CD and developer tooling.

noted.summary: SEC 8-K disclosure 2026-04-26 of March 2026 unauthorised access to internal systems; details thin, no attribution, no customer-system impact claimed.

noted.product: DRC INSIGHT Central Office Services - Content Hosting Component
noted.summary: VU#748485: /v0/configuration admin endpoint accepts unauthenticated config modifications from any on-network attacker; education-sector niche but pattern (admin+content APIs sharing one router) recurs elsewhere.
Pre-auth OS command injection CVSS 10.0 disclosed 2026-04-25 alongside Tenda F456/Fh1202 buffer overflow batch (CVE-2026-7019 → 7036, mostly 9.0); SOHO hardware, Mirai-class risk for ISP edge.
PT-2026-35197 high-severity DoS-class flaw (CVSS 7.2) disclosed 2026-04-25; minimal public detail, relevant for anyone running Technitium as a public-facing resolver.
Client-side authorization bypass — attackers forge tokens to impersonate users and control student devices; PoC published 2026-04-26, niche to K-12.

noted.product: Tropic Trooper / Earth Centaur (APT23) — trojanised SumatraPDF + GitHub C2
noted.summary: Zscaler details Chinese-speaker espionage cluster delivering custom AdaptixC2 Beacon listener via military-themed lures, then pivoting to VS Code tunnels for stealthy remote access; APAC-targeted but tradecraft is portable.
Kaspersky published research on Lua-based ICS sabotage tooling from ~2005 that predates Stuxnet; historical / strategic context for ICS-defender briefings, no current threat.

noted.product: MCP servers (chatgpt-mcp-server, Intina47 context-sync, AgentDeskAI browser-tools-mcp)
noted.summary: Three more MCP-server packages (CVSS 7.3 each) disclosed 2026-04-25/26 with classic OS command injection — pattern of unsanitised tool input into child_process.exec continues in the AI tooling ecosystem.
